name: Sonny Bono's Frutta Di Mare a La Palm Springs
recipeIngredient: 2 cans clams with juice, chopped (about 6-1/2 each)
2 cans imported Italian tomatoes with basil, chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Steam the clams and mussels over water with half the garlic, wine, and lemon juice for 9 to 10 minutes until they open. Discard any liquid.
2. Boil the crayfish in enough water to cover it, along with half the onion, carrots, a celery stalk, crushed chilies, salt, and pepper, for 15 minutes. Leave shells intact; discard the liquid.
3. Prepare the broth by cooking the remaining celery, onion, anchovies with oil, half olive oil, and bay leaves in a large covered saucepan over medium heat until the celery is fork-tender. Add clams with juice and half the wine, cooking for 5 minutes.
4. Add crushed tomatoes, oregano, basil, fennel seeds, chilies, salt, and pepper to the broth. Bring to a boil for 5 minutes.
5. Heat olive oil and butter in a large skillet over medium heat until hot. Add swordfish, snapper, scallops, shrimp, salt, and pepper. Sauté until cooked on all sides. Add remaining wine and lemon juice, cooking for 3 minutes.
6. Combine the fish with the broth, then add steamed mussels, clams, and crayfish. Serve in a deep dish with linguine, a salad of fresh greens, crisp Italian bread, and your favorite white wine.
Note: This recipe is also known as "Fruit of the Sea" and was served at Sonny Bono's restaurant in Palm Springs, CA. Formatted by Mary Wilson, BWVB02B.
